resources.string: Setting a new Material theme will reset the catalog and progress will be lost. Please confirm before proceeding.
A rock structure with its reflection mirrored over the sea
A car on a long stretch of desert road
Adaptive scaffolds provide automatic layout adjustment for different window size classes and postures.\n\nNote: this sample is best experienced in a resizable emulator or on a foldable device.
A badge can contain dynamic information, such as a new notification or the number of pending requests. Badges can contain only an icon or a short text.
A bottom app bar displays navigation and key actions at the bottom of mobile screens.
Bottom sheets are surfaces containing supplementary content, anchored to the bottom of the screen.
Buttons help people initiate actions, from sending an email to sharing a document or liking a post.
Button groups are containers for Material components that add an animation when pressed.
Cards contain content and actions that communicate information about a subject.
Carousels are stylized lists with viewing behavior suited to large images and other visually rich content.
Checkboxes let users select one or more items from a set, or turn an option on or off.
Chips let users enter information, make selections, filter content, or trigger actions.
Date pickers let users select a date or a range of dates.
Dialogs provide important prompts in a user flow. They can require an action, communicate information, or help users complete a task.
Extended FABs help people take primary actions. They are wider than FABs to accommodate a text label and a larger target area.
The FAB represents the most important action on a screen and keeps key actions within reach.
The FAB Menu displays additional key actions when a FAB is clicked.
A floating toolbar displays key actions above the content.
Icon buttons let users take actions and make choices with a single tap.
Lists are continuous, vertical indexes of text or images.
Loading indicators express an unspecified wait time or display the progress of a loading process.
Menus display a list of choices on temporary surfaces.
Navigation bars provide a persistent and convenient way to switch between primary destinations in an app.
Navigation drawers provide ergonomic access to destinations in an app.
Navigation rails provide access to primary destinations on tablet and desktop screens.

Navigation Suite Scaffold wraps the supplied content and displays the appropriate navigation component for the current NavigationSuiteType.\n\nNote: this sample is best experienced in a resizable emulator or on a foldable device.
Progress indicators express an unspecified wait time or display the progress of a process.
Pull to refresh is a swipe gesture available at the beginning of lists, grids, and card collections where the most recent content appears.
Radio buttons let users select one option from a set.
Search bars let users enter a keyword or phrase and retrieve relevant information.
Segmented buttons help people select options, switch views, or sort elements.
Toggle buttons provide selectable buttons that animate when pressed.
Sliders let users make selections from a range of values.
Snackbars provide brief messages about app processes at the bottom of the screen.
Split buttons let users perform additional actions besides the main action.
Switches toggle a single setting on or off.
Tabs organize content across different screens, data sets, and interactions.
Text fields let users enter and edit text.
Tooltips draw the user’s attention to an anchor component.
Time pickers let users choose a time of day.
Top app bars display information and actions at the top of a screen.
Material Shapes define the shapes of components.
Swipe to dismiss is a gesture used to dismiss items in a list.
